frameworks.name: adaptive gain control
frameworks.one_line: Single gain-modulation mechanism explains distraction across decision domains.

frameworks.name: divisive normalization
frameworks.one_line: Adaptive gain framing related to normalization of competing inputs.

frameworks.name: decision variable / value-based choice
frameworks.one_line: Gain-modulated decision variable indexed in dorsal cortical network.

core_question: Can a single adaptive gain-control model unify distraction effects across perceptual, cognitive and value-based decisions?
key_assumptions: Distraction shares a common computational substrate across domains
Adaptive gain modulates how irrelevant information enters the decision variable
dACC tracks the model's decision variable
key_findings: Adaptive gain model accounts for tilt illusion, conflict adaptation, and decoy effects
Model predicts counterintuitive new behaviour on flanker task variants
BOLD signals in dorsal network including dACC track gain-modulated decision variable
interpretations: Distraction across perceptual, cognitive and economic choice reflects a shared adaptive gain mechanism
dACC implements a gain-modulated decision variable predicted by the unified model
